title: There Was A Bonnie Lass.
content:
I.

            There was a bonnie lass,
            And a bonnie, bonnie lass,
        And she lo'ed her bonnie laddie dear;
            Till war's loud alarms
            Tore her laddie frae her arms,
        Wi' mony a sigh and tear.

II.

            Over sea, over shore,
            Where the cannons loudly roar,
        He still was a stranger to fear;
            And nocht could him quell,
            Or his bosom assail,
        But the bonnie lass he lo'ed sae dear.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "There Was A Bonnie Lass."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
